title: Physics Edit Proposal Schema V1
description: Schema for LLM-assisted domain physics edit proposals.  The LLM interprets a natural-language instruction into a structured patch against the current domain-physics.json.  The proposal is rendered as a form-like action card so the Domain Authority (or escalation reviewer) can accept, modify, or reject it before it enters the staging workflow.

card_type.description: Card type discriminator for physics edit proposals.

id.description: Staged command ID (UUID) — the same staged_id used in the admin HITL pipeline.

body.description: Summary of the proposed change in plain language.

target_section.description: Which top-level section of domain-physics.json is being modified.

operation_type.description: Whether the patch adds, modifies, or removes an entry.

proposed_patch.description: The structured updates dict matching what update_domain_physics expects.  Keys are top-level domain-physics fields; values are the replacement/merged content.

affected_ids.description: IDs of invariants, standing orders, or other entries affected by this patch.

current_snapshot.description: Snapshot of the target_section BEFORE the patch, for diff rendering.

diff_summary.description: Human-readable summary of what changed (generated by the LLM).

confidence.description: LLM self-reported confidence in the interpretation.

actor_id.description: Pseudonymous ID of the proposing actor.

actor_role.description: RBAC role of the proposing actor.

requires_escalation.description: True when the proposing actor is not the Domain Authority and the proposal must be escalated for approval.

escalation_record_id.description: ID of the EscalationRecord created for teacher/TA proposals (if applicable).

original_instruction.description: The natural-language instruction that triggered this proposal.

resolve_endpoint.description: API endpoint to POST for resolving this card.
